title: DOT Graph
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries Graphviz DOT language files that describe directed and undirected graphs using a declarative text syntax. DOT files are rendered to PNG, SVG, PDF, and other formats by Graphviz tools such as `dot`, `neato`, and `fdp`.

## When to use
Use `media:dot;textable` when a pipeline node produces or renders graph descriptions — for example, a dependency graph exporter that serializes a build DAG as DOT, or a rendering step that invokes `dot -Tsvg` to produce a visual output.

## Notes
The `.dot` and `.gv` extensions are interchangeable; `.gv` was introduced to avoid conflicts with Microsoft Word template files on Windows. Graphviz layout engines (`dot`, `neato`, `circo`, `twopi`) produce different spatial arrangements and should be chosen based on graph structure. See also [DOT Graph](https://capdag.com/browse/media:dot;textable).
